commonProblems.h2: Kia Picanto Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Kia Picanto delivers reliable urban mobility, but the Kappa II and Epsilon engine families have documented weak points that frequently trigger replacement searches. Timing chain degradation on the G3LA 1.0L, excessive oil consumption on the older G4HE 1.1L, and VVT actuator faults on later Kappa units are the most prominent failure patterns. Every rebuilt Kia Picanto engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost breakdowns below to decide whether a targeted repair or a full Kia Picanto engine replacement offers the best value for your situation.

problems.h4: Kia Picanto Timing Chain Failure - Why Kappa II G3LA Engines Stretch Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Kia Picanto 1.0, 1 Air, 1 Connect, 1 Plus (TA generation, 2011-2017, G3LA 1.0L 3-cylinder)
problems.rootCause: Restricted oil flow to the hydraulic tensioner due to narrow feed galleries and infrequent oil changes causes the timing chain to elongate, leading to misalignment and catastrophic valve timing loss.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Kia Picanto Timing Chain Kit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full chain, guides, tensioner and VVT actuator replacement with updated oil flush
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only before rattle becomes severe or cam phasing fails. Extends life 40k-60k miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Kia Picanto G3LA Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Bench-tested rebuilt unit with upgraded chain system, new seals, and reinforced tensioner
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent resolution. Includes 12-month warranty and eliminates repeat Kia Picanto timing chain failure risk

problems.recommendation: On a TA-generation Kia Picanto, timing chain stretch at 33,000 miles is a known design weakness. If the engine is already rattling or throwing P0014 codes, internal wear on cam lobbs and guides has likely occurred. We recommend a full Kia Picanto engine replacement in these cases, as a piecemeal chain kit rarely outlasts the weakened block components on high-mileage examples.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt G3LA Kia Picanto engine prices →

problems.h4: Kia Picanto Oil Consumption - Why SA G4HE Engines Burn Through Lubricant
problems.affectedModels: Kia Picanto 1.1, 1.1 Air, 1.1 Chill, 1.1 Plus (SA generation, 2004-2011, G4HE 1.1L 4-cylinder)
problems.rootCause: Worn piston compression rings and degraded valve stem seals allow oil to seep into the combustion chamber, burning internally without visible external leaks.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Kia Picanto Top-End Rebuild & Seal Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Valve stem seals, PCV valve, piston ring reseating and carbon clean
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if cylinder wall scoring is absent. Reduces Kia Picanto oil consumption for 20k-30k miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Reconditioned Kia Picanto G4HE Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete reconditioned unit with reinforced piston rings, updated head gasket and tested compression
repairOptions.longevity: Definitive solution for SA models over 8 years old. Restores factory oil burn rates and includes full warranty

problems.recommendation: If your SA-generation Kia Picanto is consuming more than 1 litre per 1,000 miles, the G4HE's piston rings are likely compromised beyond economical repair. Given the age and low residual value of these models, a Kia Picanto engine replacement with upgraded rings delivers the only long-term financial sense.
problems.cta: Get instant quotes for G4HE Kia Picanto engine replacement →

problems.group: Kia Picanto P0014 Camshaft Actuator Failure
problems.h4: Kia Picanto P0014 Camshaft Actuator Failure - VVT System Breakdown on Kappa Engines
problems.affectedModels: Kia Picanto 1.0 & 1.25 variants (TA & JA generations, 2011-2023, G3LA & G4LA engines)
problems.rootCause: Sludge accumulation from extended oil service intervals clogs the VVT solenoid and cam phaser, causing incorrect valve timing, rough idle and eventual limp mode.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Kia Picanto VVT Solenoid & Cam Phaser Swap
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Oil system flush, solenoid replacement, cam phaser inspection and ECU adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: Effective only if caught at first fault code. Does not address internal Kia Picanto cam wear

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Kia Picanto Kappa II Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full rebuild with new VVT components, upgraded oil pump, and bench-tested compression
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended when P0014 persists post-swap or metallic noise is present. Eliminates Kia Picanto VVT problems permanently

problems.recommendation: The P0014 code on a Kia Picanto often indicates the camshaft actuator has already suffered abrasive wear from contaminated oil. While a solenoid replacement may clear the fault temporarily, we advise fitting a complete rebuilt Kia Picanto engine if metallic tapping accompanies the warning light, as internal timing damage is irreversible.
problems.cta: Compare Kia Picanto 1.0 and 1.25 rebuilt engine costs →

problems.h4: Kia Picanto Cold Start Rattle - Timing Tensioner Weakness in T-GDi Turbo Units
problems.affectedModels: Kia Picanto GT-Line, X-Line, T-GDi trims (JA generation, 2017-present, G3LC 1.0L Turbo)
problems.rootCause: Weak primary tensioner spring design combined with turbo oil drainback causes immediate dry-start friction, creating a loud metallic rattle during cold ignition.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative - Kia Picanto Upgraded Timing Chain Kit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Factory-updated tensioner, reinforced chain rails, turbo oil feed line inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Highly recommended at first cold rattle. Prevents Kia Picanto turbo bearing starvation and extends life 50k+ miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Kia Picanto G3LC Turbo Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete turbocharged rebuild with reinforced timing components, new turbo seals and full pressure test
repairOptions.longevity: Required if engine has run with low oil pressure or seized bearings. Resolves Kia Picanto cold start rattle permanently

problems.recommendation: Early cold-start rattling on JA-generation T-GDi Kia Picanto models is a known tensioner defect that should never be ignored. If the noise lasts less than 3 seconds, an upgraded Kia Picanto timing chain kit will prevent catastrophic failure. If it persists longer or triggers misfire codes, immediate Kia Picanto engine replacement is the only safe route to protect the turbo system.
problems.cta: Compare 1.0 T-GDi Kia Picanto engine prices now →

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Kia Picanto
finalCta.paragraph: Engine problems on the Kia Picanto don't have to mean scrapping a reliable daily driver. Whether you're facing timing chain stretch on a TA G3LA, oil burning on an older SA 1.1, or VVT faults on a modern JA 1.25, EngineMarket connects you directly with verified UK specialists who price parts and labour transparently. Every rebuilt unit arrives with reinforced internal components targeting your exact failure pattern, backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes from our vetted network today and secure a Kia Picanto engine replacement that outlasts factory specifications without paying main dealer premiums.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Kia Picanto Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Kia Picanto and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Kia Picanto Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Kia Picanto across UK generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement unit.

items.title: Kia Picanto 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.0L petrol unit is the Picanto's most common engine, appearing across all three UK generations with evolving Kappa II technology. From the simple SOHC Epsilon in early SA models to the turbocharged T-GDi in JA GT-Line trims, this displacement balances urban efficiency with adequate power for city driving.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch on G3LA at low mileages; P0014 camshaft actuator codes; cold-start rattle on T-GDi units
items.cta: Compare Kia Picanto 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Kia Picanto 1.1L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.1L Epsilon engine powered entry-level SA-generation Picantos, offering straightforward naturally-aspirated performance for budget-conscious urban motoring. While reliable when maintained, these early units are now sought primarily for affordable replacement in high-mileage city cars.
commonFailurePoints: Excessive oil consumption without external leaks; head gasket failure in overheated examples
items.cta: Compare Kia Picanto 1.1L petrol engine prices

items.title: Kia Picanto 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L Kappa II four-cylinder replaced the 1.25L in JA facelift models, delivering refined smoothness and improved emissions for later Picanto variants. This updated displacement maintains the G4LA architecture while meeting stricter Euro 6 standards for urban compliance.
commonFailurePoints: VVT solenoid faults causing rough idle; camshaft actuator wear at higher mileage
items.cta: Compare Kia Picanto 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Kia Picanto 1.25L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.25L Kappa II four-cylinder was the TA generation's larger petrol option, providing stronger acceleration for motorway-capable Picanto variants. Its G4LA architecture shares components with the 1.0L Kappa II but offers greater torque for loaded urban use. Not sure which engine size your Kia Picanto needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Kia Picanto specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Camshaft actuator/VVT solenoid failure; oil consumption in high-mileage examples
items.cta: Compare Kia Picanto 1.25L petrol engine prices

items.title: Kia Picanto Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol engines power every UK Picanto across SA, TA and JA generations, with the Kappa II family dominating replacement enquiries due to timing chain and VVT-related failures.
items.descriptor: Petrol engines power every UK Picanto across SA, TA and JA generations, with the Kappa II family dominating replacement enquiries due to timing chain and VVT-related failures.
items.familiesLabel: Common Kia Picanto Petrol Engine Families
families: G3LA - 1.0L Kappa II three-cylinder (TA/JA), high-volume replacement due to timing chain stretch
G4LA - 1.25L/1.2L Kappa II four-cylinder (TA/JA), camshaft actuator faults common
G3LC/G3LE - 1.0L T-GDi turbo (JA GT-Line), cold-start rattle and turbo seal issues
G3HC - 1.0L Epsilon SOHC (SA), head gasket failure in high-mileage examples
G4HE - 1.1L Epsilon (SA), excessive oil consumption without external leaks
foundIn: JA generation (2017-present) - G3LA 1.0L, G4LA 1.2L, G3LC/G3LE 1.0L T-GDi
knownFor: Excellent urban fuel economy across all Kappa II units
G3LA timing chain stretch reported as early as 33,000 miles
P0014 camshaft timing codes on VVT-equipped Kappa engines
G4HE oil consumption driving SA-generation replacement searches

engineYears.h2: Kia Picanto Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Kia Picanto's UK production timeline from 2004 to present, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original SA-generation Picanto entered the UK market with simple, affordable Epsilon petrol engines designed for urban reliability.
keyChanges: First-generation Kia Picanto (SA) launched in UK with 1.0L and 1.1L petrol options
SOHC Epsilon engine family introduced for low-cost city motoring
Manual transmission only; no diesel or automatic variants offered in UK
knownFor: Simple, low-maintenance engines ideal for first-time drivers
G4HE 1.1L prone to oil consumption without visible leaks at higher mileage
Affordable parts and widespread independent garage familiarity
Head gasket failures reported on overheated G3HC units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2004-2007 Kia Picanto replacement engines
years.ticker: Kia Picanto 1.1 oil consumption • G4HE engine replacement cost • SA generation head gasket failure

years.description: The SA facelift refined the Epsilon engines but retained the same core architecture, keeping replacement costs low for budget-conscious owners.
keyChanges: Minor exterior and interior updates to SA generation
Emission compliance updates to G3HC and G4HE engines
Final production year for SA generation before TA replacement
knownFor: Proven reliability when oil changes are maintained on schedule
G4HE oil consumption issues become more apparent beyond 80k miles
Low vehicle values make used engine swaps financially viable
Simple engine bay layout aids independent repair accessibility

years.description: The all-new TA generation introduced the Kappa II engine family, bringing lighter three-cylinder efficiency but also new timing chain vulnerabilities.
keyChanges: Second-generation TA platform launched with revised chassis and styling
Kappa II G3LA 1.0L three-cylinder and G4LA 1.25L four-cylinder engines introduced
Improved fuel economy and emissions targeting Euro 5 compliance
knownFor: G3LA timing chain stretch reported as early as 33,000 miles due to oil starvation
G4LA offers smoother refinement but shares VVT actuator vulnerability
P0014 camshaft timing codes emerge as common diagnostic search
Lightweight Kappa II design improves urban fuel economy significantly
years.cta: Get quotes for 2011-2014 Kia Picanto replacement engines
years.ticker: Kia Picanto G3LA timing chain failure • P0014 code Kia Picanto • TA generation engine rattling noise
enquiries: Kia Picanto G3LA timing chain failure

years.preview: TA Facelift & G3LA Timing Chain Issues Emerge
years.description: The TA facelift retained the Kappa II engines but owner forums increasingly documented G3LA timing chain failures driving urgent replacement searches.
keyChanges: Minor exterior refresh and equipment updates to TA generation
G3LA and G4LA engines carried over with no fundamental redesign
Increased owner awareness of timing chain maintenance requirements
knownFor: G3LA timing chain rattle on cold start becomes widespread owner complaint
P0014 camshaft actuator codes frequently trigger check engine lights
Low vehicle values make rebuilt G3LA engines a cost-effective solution
Independent specialists develop upgraded tensioner kits for preventative fixes
years.ticker: Kia Picanto 1.0 timing chain replacement • G3LA rebuilt engine cost • Kia Picanto P0014 fix

years.description: The third-generation JA Picanto introduced turbocharged T-GDi variants alongside carry-over Kappa II naturally aspirated units, expanding performance options.
keyChanges: All-new JA platform with updated safety features and styling
G3LC/G3LE 1.0L T-GDi turbo engines introduced for GT-Line trims
Carry-over G3LA 1.0L and G4LA 1.2L naturally aspirated engines for base models
knownFor: G3LC turbo cold-start rattle reported on low-mileage examples under 10k miles
Carry-over G3LA retains timing chain vulnerability from TA generation
T-GDi variants command higher replacement costs but offer stronger performance
JA generation benefits from improved build quality and interior refinement
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2020 Kia Picanto replacement engines
years.ticker: Kia Picanto GT-Line engine replacement • G3LC cold start rattle • JA generation turbo engine cost

years.description: The JA facelift refined the T-GDi turbo engines with updated G3LE coding while retaining the core Kappa II architecture for continued affordability.
keyChanges: Exterior and interior refresh for JA facelift models
G3LE updated turbo engine code introduced for GT-Line S variants
G3LD LPG bi-fuel option available in limited UK fleet volumes
mainEngines: G3LE - 1.0L T-GDi turbo (GT-Line S, X-Line S)
knownFor: G3LE turbo benefits from revised cold-start calibration but retains chain monitoring importance
Facelift models command slightly higher rebuilt engine values due to newer registration
LPG G3LD variants attract high-mileage urban drivers seeking fuel cost savings
JA generation remains the most searched Picanto for engine replacement due to active UK parc
years.ticker: Kia Picanto GT-Line S engine • G3LE turbo replacement • JA facelift engine compatibility

faq.h2: Kia Picanto Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Kia Picanto engine?
items.answer: A rebuilt Kia Picanto engine typically costs £800-£2,400 supply-only, depending on displacement and generation. Used units start around £600 but carry no warranty. Supply and fit adds £300-£600 labour. The 1.0 G3LA and 1.25 G4LA are most affordable; turbo G3LC units cost more due to specialist rebuild requirements.
keyPoints: Used Kia Picanto engines range £600-£1,600 with limited return window only
Rebuilt G3LA or G4LA units cost £800-£2,400 with 12-month warranty
Turbo G3LC replacements run £1,200-£3,200 due to specialist machining needs
Supply and fit labour typically adds £300-£600 to engine price
Prices vary by variant, mileage, damage severity and UK supplier location
items.cta: Compare live Kia Picanto engine prices from UK specialists now

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Kia Picanto engine or should I scrap it?
items.answer: Replacing a Kia Picanto engine is worthwhile if the car is under 10 years old, has low mileage elsewhere, and replacement cost stays below £1,500. For SA-generation models over 12 years old with high mileage, scrapping often makes more financial sense unless the vehicle has sentimental or low-mileage value.
keyPoints: Kia Picanto values range £800-£3,500 depending on year, trim and condition
Rebuilt engine plus fitting rarely exceeds £2,000 for standard 1.0 or 1.25 units
JA-generation (2017+) Picantos with low mileage justify engine replacement investment
SA-generation (2004-2011) cars over 100k miles often cost more to fix than worth
Check MOT history and body condition before committing to Kia Picanto engine replacement
items.cta: Get a tailored Kia Picanto engine replacement quote in minutes

items.question: What causes the Kia Picanto 1.0 G3LA timing chain to fail?
items.answer: The Kia Picanto G3LA 1.0 timing chain fails due to restricted oil flow to the hydraulic tensioner, causing premature stretch. Poor maintenance, infrequent oil changes, and narrow feed galleries accelerate wear. Failure typically occurs between 28,000-45,000 miles, triggering rattling noises and P0014 camshaft timing codes.
keyPoints: G3LA timing chain stretch is documented on TA-generation Kia Picanto 1.0 models
Oil starvation from extended service intervals damages the hydraulic tensioner first
Cold-start rattling indicates chain slack; ignore it and valves may collide with pistons
P0014 OBD code signals camshaft timing over-advanced from chain elongation
Upgraded tensioner kits exist but full Kia Picanto engine replacement is often safer
items.cta: Compare rebuilt G3LA Kia Picanto engine prices with upgraded timing components

items.question: What does the P0014 code mean on my Kia Picanto?
items.answer: P0014 on a Kia Picanto indicates camshaft position timing over-advanced on bank 1, usually caused by VVT solenoid failure, oil sludge blocking the cam phaser, or timing chain stretch on G3LA/G4LA engines. This fault triggers limp mode and requires immediate diagnosis to prevent further Kia Picanto engine damage.
keyPoints: P0014 commonly affects Kia Picanto TA and JA models with Kappa II engines
Dirty oil or infrequent changes clog the VVT solenoid and cam phaser passages
G3LA timing chain stretch can also trigger P0014 alongside cold-start rattling
Clearing the code without fixing the root cause will see P0014 return quickly
Replacing the VVT solenoid costs £200-£400; full Kia Picanto engine rebuild may be needed
items.cta: Find a Kia Picanto specialist to diagnose your P0014 fault today

items.question: Which Kia Picanto engine is most reliable - 1.0 or 1.25?
items.answer: The Kia Picanto 1.25 G4LA four-cylinder is generally more reliable than the 1.0 G3LA three-cylinder, which suffers widespread timing chain failures. However, both Kappa II engines are robust when oil changes are maintained. For SA-generation models, the 1.1 G4HE is simple but prone to oil consumption at high mileage.
keyPoints: G4LA 1.25 Kia Picanto engines have fewer documented timing chain complaints than G3LA 1.0
Three-cylinder G3LA units experience higher stress per cylinder, accelerating timing component wear
Regular oil changes every 10k miles significantly extend life of any Kia Picanto Kappa II engine
SA-generation 1.1 G4HE engines are mechanically simple but consume oil as they age
JA-generation turbo G3LC units offer performance but require stricter maintenance for reliability
items.cta: Compare Kia Picanto 1.0 and 1.25 rebuilt engine prices and warranties

items.question: How long should a Kia Picanto engine last?
items.answer: A well-maintained Kia Picanto engine should last 120,000-150,000 miles before major work is needed. Kappa II G3LA and G4LA units often reach this milestone with regular oil changes. However, timing chain failures on G3LA engines can occur as early as 33,000 miles if maintenance is neglected, drastically shortening Kia Picanto engine lifespan.
keyPoints: Kia Picanto G4LA 1.25 engines commonly exceed 150k miles with proper servicing
G3LA 1.0 timing chain failures at low mileage are the main lifespan limiter
Oil changes every 10,000 miles or annually are critical for Kia Picanto Kappa II longevity
SA-generation Epsilon engines (G3HC, G4HE) are simple but prone to oil consumption over time
Turbo G3LC units in JA GT-Line models require stricter maintenance to reach high mileage
items.cta: Check if your Kia Picanto engine qualifies for a rebuilt replacement with warranty

items.question: Why is my Kia Picanto 1.1 burning oil with no visible leak?
items.answer: Your Kia Picanto 1.1 G4HE engine is likely burning oil internally due to worn piston rings or degraded valve stem seals. This is a known issue on SA-generation 1.1 models, with owners reporting consumption of up to 3 litres every two weeks despite no external leaks visible under the bonnet.
keyPoints: G4HE 1.1 Kia Picanto oil consumption stems from internal ring or seal wear
No external leak means oil is entering combustion chambers and burning during operation
High-mileage SA-generation Picantos (80k+ miles) are most susceptible to this issue
Topping up oil masks the problem but does not fix the underlying Kia Picanto engine wear
A rebuilt G4HE unit with upgraded rings resolves the oil consumption permanently
items.cta: Get quotes for a rebuilt G4HE Kia Picanto engine with upgraded piston rings

items.question: Can I swap a 1.25 engine into my 1.0 Kia Picanto?
items.answer: Swapping a 1.25 G4LA engine into a 1.0 G3LA Kia Picanto is technically possible but requires significant modifications to mounts, wiring, and ECU calibration. Most UK specialists advise against it due to cost, MOT compliance risks, and the fact that a rebuilt 1.0 G3LA is equally affordable and plug-and-play compatible with your Kia Picanto.
keyPoints: G3LA and G4LA Kia Picanto engines share the Kappa II family but differ in mounting points
ECU, loom, and sensor compatibility issues make 1.0-to-1.25 swaps complex and expensive
MOT emissions testing may fail if the engine swap is not properly declared and calibrated
A rebuilt G3LA Kia Picanto engine costs similar to a G4LA but fits without modification
If seeking more power, consider a JA-generation GT-Line with factory-fitted G3LC turbo instead
items.cta: Compare plug-and-play rebuilt Kia Picanto 1.0 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: Where can I buy a replacement Kia Picanto engine in the UK?
items.answer: Replacement Kia Picanto engines are available through EngineMarket's network of vetted UK specialists, offering rebuilt, reconditioned, and used units with warranty coverage. Avoid unknown online sellers; choose suppliers who provide bench-test certificates, clear warranty terms, and supply-and-fit options for your specific Picanto generation and engine code.
keyPoints: EngineMarket connects you with 100+ UK Kia Picanto engine specialists for price comparison
Rebuilt units include upgraded components addressing known G3LA timing chain or G4HE oil issues
Always request a bench-test certificate and written warranty before purchasing any Kia Picanto engine
Supply-and-fit services save time and ensure professional installation with post-fit support
Delivery is nationwide; most Kia Picanto engines dispatch within 24-48 hours of order confirmation
items.cta: Get free quotes from verified UK Kia Picanto engine suppliers in minutes

items.question: What engine code does my Kia Picanto have and how do I find it?
items.answer: Your Kia Picanto engine code is stamped on the engine block near the cylinder head or on a sticker in the engine bay. Common codes include G3LA (1.0), G4LA (1.25), G3LC (1.0 turbo), G3HC (early 1.0), and G4HE (1.1). Enter your registration above for instant identification of your exact Kia Picanto engine code and compatible replacements.
keyPoints: Kia Picanto engine codes are typically found on a sticker near the radiator or on the block
G3LA denotes 1.0L Kappa II three-cylinder; G4LA is 1.25L four-cylinder; G3LC is turbo
SA-generation codes: G3HC (1.0), G4HE (1.1); TA/JA: G3LA, G4LA, G3LC, G3LE
The VIN plate or V5C logbook may also reference your Kia Picanto engine code
Entering your registration on EngineMarket instantly matches your Picanto to compatible rebuilt engines
items.cta: Enter your reg to identify your Kia Picanto engine code and compare quotes now
